mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various AI frameworks by providing a standardized platform. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and potential learning curve for users unfamiliar with AI agent frameworks.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the Kagenti deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to the system PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster where Kagenti will be deployed. Adjust the parameters as needed.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30

```

> This command creates a GKE cluster with 3 nodes in the specified zone.
commands: gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures the kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a

```

> This command fetches the credentials for the cluster and updates the kubeconfig.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the cluster and their statuses.

steps.title: Install Kagenti
steps.description:
This step deploys Kagenti to the Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Adds the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Installs Kagenti in the specified namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies the autoscaling configuration.
> Applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment from the Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

gcloud container clusters delete kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a --quiet

```

> Uninstalls the Kagenti release from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the entire Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade Kagenti to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp kagenti-config:/path/to/config backup-config.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Backs up the current configuration.
> Upgrades the Kagenti deployment to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a' to refresh kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install chart: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm chart version may not be compatible with the current Kubernetes version.

**Fix:**
Check the compatibility and use 'helm install ... --version <compatible-version>' to specify a compatible version.

**Affected versions:** 1.26 to 1.30

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may have configuration issues or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster or adjust resource requests in the deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI agent protocols and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kagenti' showing all pods in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti
gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30
gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
